title: Deschampsia baetica
taxonomy: Deschampsia baetica is classified as a species of grass within the genus Deschampsia. It belongs to the family Poaceae. This species is further classified under the order Poales. The classification also includes the class Liliopsida and the division Magnoliophyta. It is a member of the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Deschampsia baetica has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Mediterranean region. intro: Deschampsia baetica, also known as Baetica hair grass or Baetica fescue, is a perennial grass species native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to its potential in treating conditions such as arthritis and gastrointestinal disorders. Bioactive constituents of Deschampsia baetica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs, to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
